UserProfile.title: UserProfile
UserProfile.description: Stores user's personal information, dietary preferences, health concerns, and cooking habits for personalized app experiences.

id.description: Unique identifier for the UserProfile entity.

heightCm.description: The user's height in centimeters, if provided. (Optional)

weightKg.description: The user's weight in kilograms, if provided. (Optional)

healthGoals.description: A list of health goals the user aims to achieve (e.g., 'weight loss', 'muscle gain').

dietPreferences.description: A list of dietary preferences the user follows (e.g., 'vegetarian', 'low carb', 'gluten-free').

healthConcerns.description: A list of health concerns relevant to the user's diet (e.g., 'diabetes', 'high blood pressure').

allergies.description: A list of food allergies the user has (e.g., 'peanuts', 'dairy').

foodsToAvoid.description: A list of specific foods the user wants to avoid.

preferredCuisine.description: A list of preferred cuisine types (e.g., 'Italian', 'Mexican', 'Asian').

cookingSkillLevel.description: The user's self-assessed cooking skill level (e.g., 'beginner', 'intermediate', 'expert').

householdSize.description: The number of people in the user's household, influencing recipe serving sizes.

createdAt.description: Timestamp when the user profile was created.

updatedAt.description: Timestamp when the user profile was last updated.

InventoryItem.title: InventoryItem
InventoryItem.description: Represents a single ingredient or food item tracked in the user's fridge, freezer, or pantry.

id.description: Unique identifier for the InventoryItem entity.

name.description: The name of the ingredient (e.g., 'chicken breast', 'spinach').

category.description: The category of the item (e.g., 'vegetables', 'fruits', 'meat', 'dairy', 'pantry items').

quantity.description: The quantity of the item available.

unit.description: The unit of measurement for the quantity (e.g., 'kg', 'grams', 'pieces', 'cans').

expirationDate.description: The expiration date of the item, used for waste reduction.

location.description: The storage location of the item (e.g., 'fridge', 'freezer', 'pantry').

addedAt.description: Timestamp when the inventory item was added.

updatedAt.description: Timestamp when the inventory item was last updated.

Recipe.title: Recipe
Recipe.description: Stores details for a recipe, which can be AI-generated or system-defined, including ingredients, instructions, and nutritional information.

id.description: Unique identifier for the Recipe entity.

description.description: A short description or summary of the recipe.

whyMatchHealthNeeds.description: An AI-generated explanation of why this recipe matches the user's health needs or preferences.

prepTimeMinutes.description: Estimated preparation time for the recipe in minutes.

cookTimeMinutes.description: Estimated cooking time for the recipe in minutes.

difficultyLevel.description: The estimated difficulty level of the recipe (e.g., 'easy', 'medium', 'hard').

ingredients.description: A detailed list of ingredients required for the recipe, including quantity and unit.

missingIngredients.description: A list of ingredients required by the recipe but not available in the user's inventory.

instructions.description: Step-by-step instructions on how to prepare the recipe.

estimatedCalories.description: Estimated total calories for the recipe per serving. (Optional)

estimatedCarbs.description: Estimated total carbohydrates in grams for the recipe per serving. (Optional)

estimatedProtein.description: Estimated total protein in grams for the recipe per serving. (Optional)

estimatedFat.description: Estimated total fat in grams for the recipe per serving. (Optional)

healthNotes.description: A list of health-related notes or classifications for the recipe (e.g., 'diabetic-friendly', 'low sodium', 'high protein').

substitutionSuggestions.description: General suggestions for ingredient substitutions (e.g., 'chicken breast for tofu'). (Optional)

cuisineType.description: The cuisine type of the recipe (e.g., 'Italian', 'Mexican').

generatedAt.description: Timestamp when the recipe was generated by the AI.

SavedRecipe.title: SavedRecipe
SavedRecipe.description: Links a user to a recipe they have saved, allowing them to easily access their favorite meals.

id.description: Unique identifier for the SavedRecipe entity.

savedAt.description: Timestamp when the recipe was saved by the user.

RecipeHistoryEntry.title: RecipeHistoryEntry
RecipeHistoryEntry.description: Records a user's interaction with a recipe, such as viewing, cooking, or rating it.

id.description: Unique identifier for the RecipeHistoryEntry entity.

interactionType.description: The type of interaction with the recipe (e.g., 'viewed', 'cooked', 'rated').

rating.description: The user's rating for the recipe (1-5 stars), if applicable. (Optional)

notes.description: Any additional notes the user made about this interaction. (Optional)

ShoppingListItem.title: ShoppingListItem
ShoppingListItem.description: Represents an item on the user's shopping list, which can be manually added or generated from a recipe.

id.description: Unique identifier for the ShoppingListItem entity.

name.description: The name of the item to be bought (e.g., 'milk', 'eggs').

quantity.description: The quantity of the item needed.

unit.description: The unit of measurement for the quantity (e.g., 'liters', 'dozen').

isBought.description: Indicates whether the item has been bought.

addedAt.description: Timestamp when the item was added to the shopping list.

recipeId.description: Reference to Recipe, if this item was added to the shopping list as a missing ingredient for a specific recipe. (Optional) (Relationship: Recipe 1:N ShoppingListItem)

MealPlanEntry.title: MealPlanEntry
MealPlanEntry.description: Represents a specific recipe planned by the user for a particular meal on a given day.

id.description: Unique identifier for the MealPlanEntry entity.

planDate.description: The date for which the meal is planned.

servings.description: The number of servings planned for this meal.

isCooked.description: Indicates whether this specific planned meal has been cooked.

plannedAt.description: Timestamp when this meal plan entry was created.

cookedAt.description: Timestamp when this planned meal was marked as cooked. (Optional)

Notification.title: Notification
Notification.description: Stores details about a notification to be sent or that has been sent to a user.

id.description: Unique identifier for the Notification entity.

message.description: The content of the notification message.

relatedEntityId.description: Optional reference to an entity related to the notification (e.g., an InventoryItem ID, a MealPlanEntry ID). (Optional)

scheduledTime.description: The planned time for the notification to be sent. (Optional)

sentTime.description: Timestamp when the notification was actually sent.

isRead.description: Indicates whether the user has read the notification.

definition.description: Stores user's personal information, dietary preferences, health concerns, and cooking habits. The document ID for each user profile is the user's authentication UID. This ensures direct, path-based ownership for security rules. The 'id' field within the document will match the 'userId' parameter for clarity and indexing.

params.name: userId
params.description: The unique ID of the authenticated user, which also serves as the document ID for their profile.

definition.description: Stores individual ingredients or food items belonging to a specific user. Access is restricted to the owning user via the path. The 'userId' field in the document provides a redundant link for indexing and query clarity; however, authorization relies on the path and `request.auth.uid` matching the `{userId}` wildcard.

params.name: userId
params.description: The unique ID of the authenticated user who owns this inventory item.

params.name: inventoryItemId
params.description: The unique ID of the specific inventory item.

definition.description: Contains system-defined or curated AI-generated recipes. This collection is globally readable by all authenticated users, allowing for public access and QAP-compliant listing. Write access would typically be restricted to administrators or specific backend processes. No user-specific authorization data is denormalized here as it's a shared, public resource.

params.name: recipeId
params.description: The unique ID of the recipe.

definition.description: Links a specific user to a recipe they have saved. Access is restricted to the owning user via the path. The 'userId' field in the document provides a redundant link for indexing and query clarity; however, authorization relies on the path and `request.auth.uid` matching the `{userId}` wildcard.

params.name: userId
params.description: The unique ID of the authenticated user who saved this recipe.

params.name: savedRecipeId
params.description: The unique ID of the saved recipe entry.

definition.description: Records a user's interactions with recipes (e.g., viewed, cooked, rated). Access is restricted to the owning user via the path. The 'userId' field in the document provides a redundant link for indexing and query clarity; however, authorization relies on the path and `request.auth.uid` matching the `{userId}` wildcard.

params.name: userId
params.description: The unique ID of the authenticated user whose recipe history this entry belongs to.

params.name: recipeHistoryEntryId
params.description: The unique ID of the specific recipe history entry.

definition.description: Represents an item on a user's shopping list. Access is restricted to the owning user via the path. The 'userId' field in the document provides a redundant link for indexing and query clarity; however, authorization relies on the path and `request.auth.uid` matching the `{userId}` wildcard.

params.name: userId
params.description: The unique ID of the authenticated user who owns this shopping list item.

params.name: shoppingListItemId
params.description: The unique ID of the specific shopping list item.

definition.description: Represents a specific recipe planned by a user for a particular meal on a given day. Access is restricted to the owning user via the path. The 'userId' field in the document provides a redundant link for indexing and query clarity; however, authorization relies on the path and `request.auth.uid` matching the `{userId}` wildcard.

params.name: userId
params.description: The unique ID of the authenticated user who owns this meal plan entry.

params.name: mealPlanEntryId
params.description: The unique ID of the specific meal plan entry.

definition.description: Stores details about notifications for a specific user. Access is restricted to the owning user via the path. The 'userId' field in the document provides a redundant link for indexing and query clarity; however, authorization relies on the path and `request.auth.uid` matching the `{userId}` wildcard.

params.name: userId
params.description: The unique ID of the authenticated user who receives this notification.

params.name: notificationId
params.description: The unique ID of the specific notification.

firestore.reasoning:
The recommended Firestore structure for NutriFridge AI prioritizes security, scalability, and debuggability by adhering to the core design principles. The primary strategy employed is **Structural Segregation** combined with **Path-Based Ownership** for user-specific data, and a top-level collection for globally accessible data. This design minimizes complexity in security rules and supports efficient queries.

**Authorization Independence (CRITICAL):** Authorization independence is achieved primarily through hierarchical path-based ownership for all user-specific data. For instance, `user_profiles/{userId}` and `users/{userId}/inventory_items/{inventoryItemId}` directly embed the `userId` in the document path. This means that Firestore security rules can directly check `request.auth.uid == userId` against the path wildcard (`userId`) without needing to perform `get()` operations on parent documents. This completely eliminates hierarchical authorization dependencies, making rules atomic, faster, and easier to debug. For all user-owned entities (UserProfile, InventoryItem, SavedRecipe, RecipeHistoryEntry, ShoppingListItem, MealPlanEntry, Notification), the `userId` field is also included within the document. While technically redundant for authorization in path-based rules, this denormalization provides robust indexing capabilities and clear data relationships for application logic and client-side filtering, upholding the principle of authorization independence.

**Clarity of Intent (Debuggability):** The structure clearly delineates data ownership. Paths like `/users/{userId}/...` explicitly indicate that the data within those subcollections belongs to the authenticated user identified by `{userId}`. The top-level `/recipes` collection clearly signifies a public, globally accessible resource. This explicit structure makes authorization intent immediately clear, simplifying rule writing and debugging.

**DBAC (No Custom Claims):** Roles, if needed beyond basic user ownership (e.g., for `recipes` write access by an admin), would be implemented via dedicated collections like `/roles_admin/{uid}`, where the presence of a document signifies a user's role. Authorization relies solely on `request.auth.uid` matching the path wildcard for user-owned data.

**QAPs (Rules are not Filters):** This structure inherently supports Query-As-Permissions (QAPs) by design:
*   **User-Specific Collections:** For collections like `users/{userId}/inventory_items`, `users/{userId}/saved_recipes`, etc., a user can only query their own subcollection. The security rules will allow `list` and `get` operations only if `request.auth.uid == userId`. Since the path itself enforces the user's scope, any query made by a user will naturally be limited to their data, satisfying QAPs without needing complex rule-based filtering on document content.
*   **Global Collections:** For the `/recipes` collection, all authenticated users are allowed to read (`list` and `get`). This is a homogenous security posture allowing for broad queries without requiring additional filters within the rules. If specific recipe types needed restricted access, they would be segregated into different collections (e.g., `/premium_recipes`).

**Invariants:** The structured paths ensure ownership invariants. Timestamps and other denormalized fields (like `userId` in subcollection documents) are part of the schema definitions, supporting data integrity and predictability.
